title: Move DC motor authoring from Motion keyframes to Sequencer lanes
description:
Design: docs/plans/2026-07-19-sequencer-dc-lanes-design.md

Phase 1 (browser only, no firmware change): optional per-step 'dc' map {boardId: speed} on sequence steps, clamped to +/-50. flattenDcLanes() emits targeted zero-duration ROTATE chord steps on change, driving both the bake path and the browser live sequence player. Three read-only DC lanes in the Arrange dialog. Remove dc from MOTION_TRACK_SPECS and the dead DC live-feasibility/throttle code.
notes: Hardware-verified by operator on a single-board rig (board 3 / 192.168.8.198): sq-drift1 drives servos and the DC motor in parallel from one Sequence. Bring-up required BOARDID 3 on .198, whose EEPROM held id 1 -- see the Operational note in docs/plans/2026-07-19-sequencer-dc-lanes-design.md.
close_reason: Implemented and verified end-to-end. DC lanes on Sequence steps flatten to targeted ROTATE chord steps; two firmware guards removed; pre-existing numeric-target bake bug fixed. 31 new checks in test/verify_dc_lanes.mjs, full host suite green, firmware compiles at 121344/122880 OTA bytes.
